title: Torque Converter Clutch (TCC) Diagnostic
description: Diagnoses torque converter clutch faults (P0740-P0744). Monitors TCC slip via live data and tests TCC apply via actuator control to distinguish solenoid failure from clutch material failure.
physical_solution: Monitor TCC slip RPM (engine RPM minus transmission input RPM) in live data. At highway cruise, TCC should be fully locked (slip = 0-30 RPM). Test TCC solenoid resistance. Fluid contamination and clutch material wear require rebuild.

uds_services: $22 ReadDataByIdentifier (TCC slip RPM, TCC duty cycle)
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — Engine RPM via Mode $01 PID $0C universally accessible. TCC slip calculation requires transmission input shaft RPM via OEM PID ($22 TCM address). $2F TCC apply command accessible on GM pre-2017. Most TCC diagnosis can be done with engine RPM + VSS + careful load monitoring.
preconditions: Transmission fluid at correct level and not burned
No active misfire DTCs (would mask TCC diagnosis)
Record at highway cruise: 45-65mph, light throttle

procedure_steps.description: Read engine RPM: Mode $01 PID $0C. Read vehicle speed: Mode $01 PID $0D. Calculate approximate gear ratio. At highway cruise in top gear with TCC locked, engine RPM should be consistent with gear ratio × wheel speed.

Read TCC slip RPM via OEM PID: $22 [TCC_SLIP_DID] to TCM address. Normal locked TCC: 0-30 RPM slip. Shuddering TCC: 50-200 RPM oscillating slip. Failed to lock: slip equals full converter slip (150-400 RPM).

Read TCC duty cycle: $22 [TCC_DUTY_DID]. TCC solenoid at 100% duty = fully applied (locked). At 0% = unlocked. Oscillating duty during shudder = controlled TCC slip strategy (normal on some OEMs) vs actual shudder.

TCC SHUDDER vs VIBRATION DIAGNOSIS: Shudder occurs at specific speed (40-55mph), disappears above 60mph, worse when cold. Brake to 35mph (disengages TCC) — shudder stops = TCC issue confirmed. Vibration present at all speeds = driveline issue, not TCC.

TCC SOLENOID TEST: With transmission pan off, measure TCC solenoid coil resistance. Spec: 10-15 ohms typical. Open circuit or short = solenoid replacement. Electrical tests normal but P0741 = clutch material or valve body issue.

TCC apply test (GM older): $2F [TCC_CONTROL_DID] 03 [APPLY]. Commands TCC fully on. Monitor slip drops to 0 RPM = solenoid and hydraulics functional. No slip change = hydraulic issue or failed clutch.
After repair: road test at 50-60mph steady cruise. TCC slip should be 0-30 RPM, no shudder, fuel economy returned to normal. P0740-P0744 should not return after 3 drive cycles.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: A750F/A960E transmissions: TCC apply at 35-40mph depending on engine load. Techstream shows TCC slip RPM directly. Common: TCC shudder on A750F with degraded ATF. Toyota Type T-IV or WS fluid — never Dexron.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: 9AT/10AT: TCC slip monitoring via HDS. Honda-specific TCC diagnosis: shudder often indicates transmission fluid degradation (DW-1 or Z-1). Fluid change + relearn often resolves shudder without rebuild on Honda.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: RE5R05A/7AT: TCC accessible via CONSULT. Nissan Matic-S or D fluid. VQ35DE: TCC shudder common at 60k+ miles — fluid change often resolves. CONSULT shows TCC duty cycle directly.
oem_notes.Ford: 6R80: TCC slip via IDS. Ford Mercon LV fluid mandatory (Mercon V/Dexron causes TCC shudder). TCC shudder on 6R80 extremely common — fluid change with Mercon LV often resolves P0741. ELM327: Mode $01 RPM + VSS for indirect diagnosis.
oem_notes.GM: 6L80/8L90: TCC known issues. GM Dexron HP fluid. 6L80 TCC shudder extremely common on 5.3L trucks — GM TSB recommends fluid change + TC replace on severe cases. GDS2 for TCC slip monitoring. ELM327 $2F on pre-2017.
oem_notes.BMW: ZF 8HP: TCC-equivalent is internal clutch — not traditional TCC. BMW uses Lepelletier gear set. No P0740 on ZF 8HP typically. BMW ATF (Esso LT71141 or similar). No traditional TCC diagnostic applies.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: DSG 0AM/0GC: dry or wet dual-clutch, NOT torque converter. No TCC. P0740 on DSG: clutch actuator or mechatronics. Tiptronic/Aisin-sourced ATs on older models: standard TCC. VW ATF G-055-025-A2.
oem_notes.Stellantis: 845RE/68RFE: TCC slip via wiTECH. Mopar ATF+4 fluid. Hemi 5.7L: TCC shudder common with aftermarket torque converters. 68RFE on Ram diesel: TCC issues under tow loads. wiTECH for duty cycle monitoring.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: 8AT/8DCT: TCC monitoring via GDS2. Hyundai Genuine ATF SP-IV. Common: 8AT TCC shudder on 2015-2019 Sonata/Tucson — fluid change + relearn often resolves. ELM327 Mode $01 for indirect RPM monitoring.
